message: Diagram lists 'full-surface.js', 'encoder-surface.js', 'validation.js' as if directly under doe-gpu src, but they live at 'src/vendor/webgpu/shared/'. Unqualified names may mislead contributors.
resolution: Diagram now shows fully qualified paths under src/vendor/webgpu/shared/.

message: Diagram shows 'Bun FFI (bun-ffi.js)' as parallel to N-API addon, but src/bun.js conditionally loads either bun-ffi.js (Linux) or the full surface. The diagram oversimplifies the conditional path.
resolution: Diagram rewritten to show 'or Bun FFI bridge (src/vendor/webgpu/bun-ffi.js via src/bun.js)' acknowledging the conditional path.

message: Prose says browser.js 'wraps the browser's own navigator.gpu behind the same doe-gpu JS classes'. browser.js actually creates its own surface classes (createFullSurfaceClasses, createBrowserSurfaceClasses) and wraps native objects rather than simply forwarding.
resolution: Rewritten to 'wraps browser-native WebGPU objects behind Doe surface classes'.

message: Quirk directory shown as 'quirk/' with 'runtime.zig', 'quirk_json.zig', 'toggle_registry.zig' but omits the actual entry point 'quirk/mod.zig' and 'quirk_actions.zig'.
resolution: Diagram now lists mod.zig and quirk_actions.zig.

message: Backend file counts overstated ~2x. Diagram says metal/ ~70, vulkan/ ~60, d3d12/ ~50. Actual counts are approximately 34, 26, and 18 respectively.
resolution: File counts removed from diagram entirely. Backend boxes now show descriptive labels only.

message: core/ annotations omit 'replay/' which exists under core/. Also core/ has 'surface.zig' which could confuse readers since diagram implies surface is full/-only.
resolution: Diagram now lists replay/ and surface.zig under core/.

message: full/lifecycle/ annotation says '(device)' but it actually contains async diagnostics (wgpu_async_diagnostics_command.zig), not device lifecycle.
resolution: Annotation now reads 'lifecycle/ (surface + async diagnostics)'.

message: model_*.zig list omits several files: model_binding_value_types.zig, model_runtime_types.zig, model_surface_types.zig, model_texture_format_value_types.zig, model_texture_layout_value_types.zig, model_texture_value_types.zig, model_transfer_types.zig, model_webgpu_types.zig. Most are compatibility barrels but they exist.
resolution: Diagram now reads 'model_*.zig (split leaf contracts + compatibility barrels)'.

message: References 'tools/check_core_import_fence.py' but this file does not exist in the repository. Dangling reference.
resolution: File exists at runtime/zig/tools/check_core_import_fence.py. README references correct path.

message: 'plan executors' in entry points box is vague. Should name specific files (main_doe_plan_executor.zig, main_dawn_plan_executor.zig).
resolution: Entry points box now names main_doe_plan_executor.zig and main_dawn_plan_executor.zig.

message: 'Command = CoreCommand ∪ FullCommand (comptime-verified)' accurately matches model_commands.zig lines 72-104 comptime verification block.
core/full as 'command partition, not layers' description is consistent with docs/architecture.md.
Zig 0.15.2 requirement matches the build cache zig_version_string.
Claims 'No default exports in internal modules' but many internal vendor modules use default exports.
resolution: Rewritten: 'Avoid new default exports in authored internal modules. Vendor-synced compatibility modules may retain upstream defaults, and package entrypoints may use either.'

message: Lists 'assertEnum' as a runtime type check helper. No function named assertEnum exists.
resolution: Replaced with actual helpers: assertObject, assertArray, assertBoolean, assertNonEmptyString, assertIntegerInRange, assertOptionalIntegerInRange, normalizeEnumKey.

message: Import example paths did not correspond to actual import statements.
resolution: Examples now use correct paths: vendor/doe-namespace.js, vendor/webgpu/shared/resource-lifecycle.js, vendor/webgpu/shared/validation.js.

message: Claims file headers should be '2-3 line // comment'. Several files use 1-line headers.
resolution: Changed to '1-3 line'.

message: Claims 'Named exports at end of file for internal modules'. Not consistently followed.
resolution: Rewritten: 'Prefer named exports grouped at the end of authored modules. Small facades, package entrypoints, and vendor-synced modules may export inline when that is clearer.'

message: Does not specify a string quoting convention (single vs double).
resolution: Added: 'Prefer single quotes in authored package code. Follow the surrounding file's existing quote style in vendor-synced or generated modules.'

message: Says 'Throw Error directly; do not subclass' but compiler-errors.js attaches custom properties for discrimination.
resolution: Added: 'Attaching stable machine-readable fields to Error objects is acceptable when callers need structured diagnostics.'

message: Claims tests use 'check(label, condition)' but actual signature includes optional detail parameter.
resolution: Updated to 'check(label, condition, detail?)'.

message: 'Line length: prefer under 100 characters.' Multiple validation.js lines exceed 100 chars. Advisory phrasing makes this non-blocking but actual practice diverges.
resolution: Advisory wording is intentional; no change needed.

message: 'Zero runtime dependencies' confirmed: package.json has no 'dependencies' field.
'type: module' in package.json confirmed at line 6.
Claim 'No snake_case in config fields' contradicted by numerous files.
resolution: Rewritten: 'camelCase for new JSON object keys. Existing snake_case fields are part of the on-disk contract in several registries and trace outputs; do not rename them without a migration.'

message: Claim 'All schemas use additionalProperties: false' violated by schemas that intentionally allow extension.
resolution: Rewritten: 'Most schemas use additionalProperties: false. When a schema intentionally allows extension, say so explicitly in the schema and migration notes.'

message: Content version described as 'ISO 8601 date or semantic identifier' was misleading about hybrid formats.
resolution: Rewritten: 'stable human-readable identifier. It may be date-stamped, semantic, or a mixed release label such as 2026-03-29-execution-profiles-v1.'

message: Canonical serialization 'json.dumps(value, indent=2, sort_keys=True)' not consistently used across all scripts.
resolution: Scoped to 'canonical serialization for emitted config artifacts' rather than all JSON output.

message: 'Every config file has a corresponding .schema.json' was aspirational.
resolution: Rewritten: 'Every hand-authored config file should have a corresponding .schema.json; legacy and generated artifacts are documented separately.'

message: Cross-reference patterns confirmed: sourcePolicyPath in backend-lane-map.json, triggerPolicyId in numeric-stability-policy.json.
schemaVersion as integer with const constraint confirmed across all sampled schemas.
config/migration-notes.md exists and is actively maintained.
Claims 'Google-style with triple double quotes' but codebase uses NumPy-style docstrings.
resolution: Changed to 'NumPy-style with triple double quotes.'

message: 'from __future__ import annotations at the top of every file.' Not universally followed.
resolution: Changed to 'in new files and keep it at the top when present.'

message: 'Guard with raise SystemExit(main()).' Multiple files use sys.exit(main()) instead.
resolution: Changed to 'Prefer if __name__ == "__main__": raise SystemExit(main()) for new scripts.'

message: 'Line length: 80 characters max.' Not enforced and routinely exceeded.
resolution: Changed to 'prefer 80 characters in new and touched files; the tree is not uniformly enforced yet.'

message: 'pathlib.Path over os.path for all path operations.' Legacy files still use os.path.
resolution: Changed to 'Prefer pathlib.Path for new code; legacy files still use os.path in a few places.'

message: 'Never catch bare Exception unless re-raising.' check-health.py catches bare Exception without re-raising.
resolution: Changed to 'Prefer specific exception types. Bare Exception still appears in a few legacy aggregation paths that need to keep processing, but it should be avoided in new code.'

message: 'Parse arguments in a separate parse_args() function.' check-health.py has no parse_args().
resolution: Changed to 'Most entry points parse arguments in a separate parse_args() function, but small legacy helpers may inline CLI setup.'

message: Claims scripts use kebab-case but several use snake_case.
resolution: Added: 'Imported utility modules may stay snake_case.py even when they are also runnable as scripts.'

message: Covers 'bench/, pipeline/, and scripts/' but does not mention runtime/zig/tools/.
resolution: Added: 'and Python helper tooling under runtime/zig/tools/.'

message: 'Use absolute imports. No relative imports.' Verified: no relative imports found. Accurate.
'unittest.TestCase class-based tests.' All 43+ test files use unittest. No pytest found. Accurate.
'1200 lines max per Python file.' Matches AGENTS.md line 204 and is enforced by check-health.py (PYTHON_MAX_LINES = 1200).
Modern union syntax (str | None, list[str], dict[str, Any]) confirmed across bench/. No Optional[] usage found.
Claims inductive constructors use '.snake_case' but convention is mixed.
resolution: Rewritten: 'follow the existing constructor style in the file. Many single-word constructors use lowercase tags; multiword constructors often keep camelCase spelling.'

message: Claims 'Lean version is pinned in config/toolchains.json and lean-toolchain' but no lean-toolchain file exists.
resolution: Changed to 'pinned in config/toolchains.json and the repo build/check scripts.'

message: Claims Generated/ files are 'produced by pipeline/lean/scripts/' but that directory does not exist.
resolution: Changed to 'produced by pipeline/lean/generate_comparability_contract.py. The proof artifact itself is emitted by pipeline/lean/extract.sh.'

message: Claims re-export shims should be 'one-line imports' but Doe/Model.lean is 37 lines.
resolution: Changed to 'Keep them thin when practical; some legacy shims are longer because they bridge staged migrations.'

message: States 'Every theorem must carry a classification comment' but most files lack them.
resolution: Changed to 'should carry' (advisory, not mandatory).

message: Module structure diagram omits many Core/ and Shader/ files.
resolution: Added: 'This diagram is representative, not exhaustive; it names the main module families without listing every file.'

message: Claims 'Build via shell scripts in pipeline/lean/' but the referenced scripts/ directory does not exist.
resolution: Changed to 'Build via pipeline/lean/check.sh and pipeline/lean/extract.sh with explicit LEAN_PATH.'

message: File header format prescribed but foundational files have no file header.
resolution: Added: 'Some foundational legacy files still omit file headers; add them when those files are edited for substantive work.'

message: Theorem naming claims 'camelCase' but codebase uses both camelCase and snake_case.
resolution: Rewritten to acknowledge both: 'camelCase is common in Core/; snake_case also appears in shader/bounds theorem families.'

message: calc block example matches ComputeBounds.lean lines 53-58 exactly.
toggleAlwaysSupported tactic mode example matches Dispatch.lean lines 8-11 exactly.
Extraction claims verified: Doe/Extract.lean, pipeline/lean/extract.sh, and config/proof-artifact.schema.json all exist.
All 5 linked STYLE.md files exist. No broken references. Surrounding sections flow cleanly.
No remaining references to removed Naming (Zig), Naming (JavaScript), or Naming (config/JSON) subsections anywhere in AGENTS.md.
Reference to 'Package layer stack' diagram in root README.md is now accurate. Section exists at README.md line 25.
Public package exports omit './hybrid' even though src/hybrid.js and src/hybrid.d.ts exist.
resolution: ./hybrid export added to package.json exports map.

message: The stated 999-line limit is currently exceeded by runtime/zig/src/doe_wgsl/ir_transform_robustness_test.zig (1381 lines), runtime/zig/src/doe_wgsl/mod_api_test.zig (1285), and runtime/zig/src/doe_wgsl/shader_emit_test.zig (1015). The guide reads as current policy, but the tree is not fully compliant.
resolution: Tracked. These three test files need sharding per AGENTS.md line-limit policy.

message: The build-cache section ignores nested Zig caches but omits the active repo-root .zig-cache/ and .zig-cache-local/ directories.
resolution: .zig-cache/ and .zig-cache-local/ entries added to .gitignore. Note: previously tracked cache artifacts remain in git index and need a separate git rm --cached cleanup.
